rationale: The cited paper is used in the hypothesis-development section to support a core conceptual claim: free (vs. paid) offerings reduce commitment and make outcome benefits less certain. That claim directly underpins the citing paper's boundary-condition prediction (H3) about free versus paid education and is then tested empirically in Study 4. So the citation is not a passing background mention; it meaningfully informs the paper's theorizing about when its main effect should attenuate.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Datta et al. (2015)'s insight about free-trial offerings and reduced outcome certainty/commitment to the education context. It uses that prior finding to justify and test a free-vs-paid moderation of its PDB by message-appeal effect.
